name: Trilogía de San Vicente
description:
The trilogy starts in Aínsa and takes you to three great singletrack descents, all of which are located near the town of San Vicente. The names of these paths are: Planaermita, Cocullón and Mayorcas. They are entertaining, fast, rocky and really fun. There is a fountain in San Vicente where you can fill up your water along the way. The route is suitable for everyone who likes moderately difficult singletrack descents and likes to complete the climbs on wide gravel paths and asphalted side roads.

If you are looking for a particularly challenging single trail tour, then you can add the “Triología de San Vicente” to the Peñas Cinglas and Coda Sartén tours (routes numbers 5 and 18). But be careful, this mountain bike tour is really tough. All uphills are extremely strenuous and will really challenge you, we promise! This mega round is almost 47 kilometers and 1,850 meters in altitude and - depending on your fitness - takes around five to six hours.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aínsa-Sobrarbe offers diverse terrain in the foothills of the Pyrenees. The region is characterized by its mountainous landscapes, deep valleys, and extensive forests, providing a challenging and varied environment for mountain bikers. Numerous trails wind through this area, featuring a mix of technical descents and demanding climbs. The geological formations and natural beauty of the Sobrarbe Geopark contribute to the unique character of the routes. Best mountain bike trails around Aínsa-Sobrarbe The most popular mountain bike trail is Bajo Peñas (variant), a 20.3 miles (32.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ruta Bajo Peñas – circular desde Aínsa (ZZ-007), a difficult 15.7 miles (25.2 km) path. This circular route starts from Aínsa and offers panoramic views of the surrounding valleys. Local mountain bikers also love the San Vicente Trilogy v2.0 Trail, a 15.9 miles (25.6 km) trail leading through mountainous forest and open sections, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Mountain biking around Aínsa-Sobrarbe is defined by its Pyrenean foothills, river valleys, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to technically demanding singletrack. The routes in Aínsa-Sobrarbe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 6000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aínsa-Sobrarbe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Aínsa-Sobrarbe?
A: Aínsa-Sobrarbe offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 100 routes available. These range from easier paths to technically demanding singletrack, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Aínsa-Sobrarbe?
A: Yes, Aínsa-Sobrarbe has trails suitable for beginners and families. While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are 11 easy routes and 26 moderate routes that offer a less demanding experience, allowing riders to enjoy the scenery without excessive technical difficulty.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Aínsa-Sobrarbe?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Aínsa-Sobrarbe is generally during spring and autumn. The weather is milder, and the trails are typically in good condition. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winter may bring snow to higher elevations, limiting access to some routes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Aínsa?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970611765/ruta-bajo-penas-circular-desde-ainsa-zz-007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta Bajo Peñas – circular desde Aínsa (ZZ-007)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that starts and ends in Aínsa, offering panoramic views of the surrounding valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Aínsa-Sobrarbe?
A: The terrain in Aínsa-Sobrarbe is diverse, characterized by the foothills of the Pyrenees. You can expect a mix of mountainous landscapes, deep river valleys, and extensive forests. Trails often feature technical descents, demanding climbs, and varied surfaces, including rocky sections and forest paths.

Q: Are there advanced or difficult mountain bike trails in Aínsa-Sobrarbe?
A: Absolutely. Aínsa-Sobrarbe is well-known for its challenging mountain biking. There are 70 difficult routes, offering significant elevation gain and technical sections. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1138225300/bajo-penas-variant" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bajo Peñas (variant)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970648139/san-vicente-trilogy-v2-0-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Trilogy v2.0 Trail&lt;/a&gt; are popular among experienced riders seeking demanding climbs and thrilling descents.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike trails with good viewpoints in Aínsa-Sobrarbe?
A: Many trails in the region offer stunning views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1138256252/viewpoints-of-ainsa-and-morillo-by-cerro-coton" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoints of Aínsa and Morillo by Cerro Cotón&lt;/a&gt; route is specifically designed to showcase panoramic vistas. Additionally, highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7584863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Cotón Trail and Reservoir View&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3576844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mipanas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; are accessible from various routes and provide excellent photo opportunities.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Aínsa-Sobrarbe?
A: The mountain bike trails in Aínsa-Sobrarbe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, challenging climbs, and the stunning natural beauty of the Sobrarbe Geopark.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4594760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aínsa Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, which is a highlight in itself, or visit historical structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Samitier Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446024" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saints Emeterius and Celedonius&lt;/a&gt;. These sites often provide a fascinating break during your ride.

Q: Are there any lakes or natural pools accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area features beautiful natural water bodies. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3537082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martin Pools&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1208912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basacol Pools&lt;/a&gt;, which offer refreshing spots to visit. Some routes may pass close to these natural attractions, providing opportunities for a scenic stop.

Q: What is the typical duration for a mountain bike trail in Aínsa-Sobrarbe?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Aínsa-Sobrarbe varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, a difficult route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1138208018/trilogia-de-san-vicente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trilogía de San Vicente&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 48 minutes, while others can be shorter or extend to several hours for more extensive tours.
tips: A charming little town, there is a fountain to cool off.
On this gravel road, which is located near San Vicente, you have a great view. It's best to just stop for a moment and enjoy.
The Camino de Cocullón is one of three great single trails that you can take on in the “Triología de San Vicente”. The route is quick, not too difficult and just plain fun.

Small circular temple from the year 1665 that shelters the cross that commemorates the reconquest of Aínsa by the Christian warriors. Within the popular narrative of Muslim exile, this cross has its own legend.
